_raw_content: Every unfelt emotion had to go somewhere. It didn't leave. It became structure. It became your posture, your tone, your preferences, your limits. What you call your personality is mostly preserved survival. The emotions you couldn't process didn't disappear — they calcified into the identity you now defend.
slide_5_revelation: The emotions you couldn't process became the personality you think is you.

slide_6.text: Your nervous system stored what it couldn't complete. Then it built a self around the storage. You didn't choose your identity. Your overwhelm did.

caption:
You felt something too big.

Your system froze it in place.

Then you forgot it was ever there.

And slowly — without permission — it became you.

Your reactions. Your rigidity. Your voice. Your walls.

Not personality. Preservation.

Not identity. Incomplete processing.

The moment you see this, something ancient in you exhales.

Because you finally understand — you are not your armor.

You are what exists beneath it.

Still intact. Still waiting.
